description_long: This object consists of a group of eight art books, seven of which are standing upright in a slightly staggered row, while one lies flat in front. The covers of the books feature prominent, colorful artwork, each representing a different artist or art style, with the most visible being a portrait of Frida Kahlo and the iconic wave by Hokusai. The books have white or light-colored covers and spines, with large, clear title text and some visible artwork on the spines. The books appear to be standard hardcover or softcover art books, with glossy printed covers and visible pages. The overall arrangement suggests a curated collection, likely intended for display or easy browsing. The books are of uniform size, giving the group a neat, organized appearance.
description: A neatly arranged group of eight art books, each with colorful cover artwork and white spines, including a prominent portrait and a famous wave illustration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the staggered alignment of the seven upright books and the single book lying flat in front.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the colorful cover illustrations, including a portrait and a wave, with all book titles visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the thickness and uniformity of the books, with the flat book's cover partially visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain, mostly white backs of the books with minimal detail.
attribute_cover_illustration: The main cover illustration features a painted portrait of a woman with dark hair, adorned with a white floral headdress and a neutral background. The style is realistic and expressive, capturing the subject's facial features and attire in detail.
attribute_spine_design: The spines are white with large, uppercase black text running vertically, each displaying a different artist's name. Some spines also feature a small section of the cover artwork near the top.
